question: Write a program that takes a character input from the user. Check if the character is an uppercase vowel (A, E, I, O, U) using a `switch` statement, and print whether it is or not.

test_cases.expected: It is not an uppercase vowel.
It is not an uppercase vowel.

driver: int main() { char input_char; std::cin >> input_char; switch (input_char) { case 'A': case 'E': case 'I': case 'O': case 'U': std::cout << "It is an uppercase vowel.\n"; break; default: std::cout << "It is not an uppercase vowel.\n"; break; } return 0; }
errors: The switch statement uses the expression 'ch - 'A'', which evaluates to an integer difference. The case labels are still character literals ('A', 'E', etc.), which are their ASCII integer values. This mismatch means the case labels will never correctly match the switch expression's result for actual vowels, causing all inputs to fall to the default case.
